rulings.Case Title: PAUL A. VANDER WAERDT VS. CHARLES M. COLLINS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 16, 2008, line 17, PLAINTIFF PAUL VANDER WAERDT'S Motion To Amend Complaint IS GRANTED IN PART TO PLEAD A CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST COLLINS BASED UPON COLLINS' REPRESENTATION IN THE MANN UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTIONS. AS A MATTER OF LAW PLAINTIFF CANNOT SEEK EMOTIONAL DISTRESS DAMAGES. rulings.Case Title: PATRICK DUCKWALD et al VS. HANUS JELINEK et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 16, 2008, line 5, CROSS-COMPLAINANT HANUS JELINEK MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THE EXISTENCE OF A LANDLORD-TENANT RELATIONSHIP FOR THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION. THE PARTIES' COMPLICATED ARRANGEMENT CAN BE CHARACTERIZED AS A CONTINUATION OF THE TENANCY IN COMMON RELATIONSHIP. JELINEK HAS SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE ON THE EJECTMENT ACTION AND DUCKWALD HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE OF A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT BASED ON JELINEK'S ALLEGED FAILURE TO PERFORM THE TRUST AGREEMENT. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK DUCKWALD et al VS. HANUS JELINEK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate Claims For Ejectment And Unlawful Detainer Or, In The Alt, To Bifurcate The Issue Of Right To Possession Of 789 Buena Vista Avenue, #102 From Claims For Ejectment And Unlawful Detainer;

THIS IS REALLY A MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION WITHOUT THE REQUISITE SHOWING. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH ACCESS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issuance Of Peremtory Writ Of Mandate And Injunctive Relief; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 16, 2008, line 11, PETITIONER MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH ACCESS Motion For Issuance Of Peremtory Writ Of Mandate And Injunctive Relief. The writ is granted. The decision of the United States Supreme Court in Memorial Hospital v. Maricopa County (1974) 415 U.S. 250, 262 makes clear that a "durational residence requirement for free medical care penalizes indigents for exercising their right to migrate to and settle in [California]. Accordingly, the classification created by the residence requirement, 'unless shown to be necessary to promote a compelling state interest, is unconstitutional.'" Memorial Hospital is just one decision in a long line of Supreme Court decisions striking down such requirements. The State has not offered a reason consistent with the mandates of the constitution to justify the resident requirement. Accordingly, the writ is granted and Insurance Code section 12698(a) is declared unconstitutional. =(302/PJM/VC)
